disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe gripping pain high under the right ribs, radiating up to the right shoulder blade, that began about 45-60 minutes after a fatty meal (burger and chips)
trigger_question_examples: Where is the pain and does it spread anywhere?
What were you doing just before it started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's up high on the right under my ribs and it shoots into my right shoulder blade. It hit me about an hour after I ate a burger and chips.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe colicky, wave-like pain with marked restlessness — the patient paces and cannot stay still or find a comfortable position
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the pain and does anything ease it?
Can you stay still or do you keep moving?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes in waves and when it peaks it's unbearable — I can't sit or lie still, I just pace around.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe nausea and one episode of vomiting (food/fluid, non-bloody); unable to keep any oral intake down
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt sick or been vomiting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel really sick and I threw up once. I can't keep any food or drink down while the pain is like this.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Prior similar but milder episodes that resolved on their own within 3-4 hours
trigger_question_examples: Have you had pain like this before?
What happened with those earlier episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've had milder versions of this a few times before, usually after rich food. They always went away on their own in a few hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Oral paracetamol taken about an hour ago with no effect on the pain
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken anything for the pain and did it help?
Did the paracetamol make any difference?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I took a couple of paracetamol about an hour ago and it did absolutely nothing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, shivering or shaking chills reported by the patient
trigger_question_examples: Any fevers, shivering or shaking chills?
Do you feel hot or feverish at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I don't feel hot and I haven't had any shivering or chills.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't taken my temperature, but I don't feel feverish.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No yellowing of the eyes or skin, and no dark urine or pale stools reported
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any yellowing of your eyes or skin, or dark urine?
Have your stools looked pale or your urine looked dark?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my eyes and skin look normal to me and my urine and stools look the same as usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting of blood or coffee-ground material, and no black tarry or bloody stools
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited any blood or seen black or bloody stools?
Was there anything like coffee grounds in the vomit?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood in the vomit and no black or bloody stools.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No central crushing chest pain, no breathlessness and no sweating radiating to the arm or jaw
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, breathlessness, or pain spreading to your arm or jaw?
Do you feel any pressure or tightness in your chest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain and no trouble breathing — it's really just the right side under my ribs and up to my shoulder blade.
